key_insight: PREDIKTOR improves therapeutic response prediction by aligning a patient-specific knowledge graph with a simulated drug-induced transcriptomic perturbation view.
clinical_relevance: This could help identify which cancer patients are more likely to benefit from a given drug before treatment, with added interpretability for mechanism-focused decision support.
method: Builds an individualized gene regulatory network from each patient’s tumor expression and adds drug-target links from DrugBank.
Uses a graph neural encoder for a mechanistic patient-drug embedding and a frozen LINCS-pretrained model to simulate post-perturbation gene expression.
Aligns both views in a shared latent space with contrastive learning, then combines them for response classification.
evidence_signal: On TCGA, the model outperformed state-of-the-art baselines under patient-, drug-, and tissue-split tests and transferred zero-shot to I-SPY2, improving AUROC by 5.6%.
watch_item: Because the approach relies on pretrained perturbation models and inferred networks, performance may depend on data quality and external validation before clinical use.
paper_title: Predicting Therapeutic Outcome via Aligning Patient-Specific Knowledge Graph and Gene-Level Perturbation Representations
